t_ch: 但那声音稍纵即逝。你尝试着回溯到记忆的前一帧，却发现手中的胶片已经被切断了，切口干净利落，不留任何痕迹。
t_en: But the voice passes rapidly. You try to recall what you saw in those fleeting moments, but find no trace.

t_ch: 然后，你又一次听到了女人的声音……
t_en: All of a sudden, you hear it again... a female voice in your ear...

t_ch: 「在旅途的尽头，所有困扰你的谜题都将会解开。」
t_en: "At the end of your journey, all that perplexes you and troubles you will resolve."

t_ch: ——对啊，当时可真是千钧一发！好在我桑博灵机一动：嘿！我又没必要去担任那个主角，是不是？咱们当好戴面具的丑角就对了嘛。
t_en: Aw, it was a close thing alright! Good thing it dawned on me: Hey, Sampo doesn't have to be a protagonist, right? The likes of us are better suited to... shadowy comic relief, huh?

t_ch: 事实证明，星穹列车这伙人比我想得还要有趣！你很喜欢？当真？哈哈哈，真是杰作啊。
t_en: Turns out the Astral Express crew is more fun than I expected! You liked it? Really? Haha, told you so!

t_ch: …不不，我还不想离开这儿。什么，艾普瑟隆？那种纸醉金迷的地方有什么乐子好找？「越能体现人性尊严的快乐，越是高级的快乐」——这才有趣！
t_en: ...No no, I'm in no hurry to leave. What? Oh, Epsilon? Pff. What fun can you get out of a giant vanity fair like that? "True happiness always entails the manifestation of the dignity of mankind..." Now that's a quote I live by.

t_ch: 好，拜啦。替我向酒馆的兄弟姐妹们问好，别忘了告诉他们——就说敬请期待我老桑博的下一场戏！现在我要谢幕啦。
t_en: Well, bye for now. Send my regards to the guys and girls in the tavern. Oh, and don't forget to say that Sampo Koski will catch them for the next one. Time to make my curtain call.

t_ch: 这个故事敬献给您，亲爱的看官——不知您是否从中得到了少许欢愉？
t_en: To you, my dear audience, I dedicate my performance... I wonder, did I bring a little more joy into your lives?

t_ch: ——如果您说没有，啊，桑博会很伤心喔。
t_en: ...You don't have to answer that — but if the answer is no, then you'll break my heart.

t_ch: （该回列车了，去找三月和丹恒吧。）
t_en: We should be getting back to the Express. Time to find March and Dan Heng.

t_ch: 早上好呀，{NICKNAME}！睡得怎么样？我现在可是精神满满！
t_en: Morning, {NICKNAME}! How did you sleep? I'm full of energy myself!

t_ch: 精神满满！
t_en: I'm full of energy myself!

t_ch: …我明白了，又开始复读了是吧。
t_en: ...Copycatitis still has no cure I see.

t_ch: 好像梦到了桑博…
t_en: I dreamed about Sampo...

t_ch: 噫，真晦气！我去翻翻黄历，没准今天不宜出行。
t_en: You poor thing! I'll go check the lunar calendar — today might be unlucky.

t_ch: 该回家了！
t_en: It's time we went home!

t_ch: 真舍不得这宾馆的床呀…好松好软好舒服！
t_en: *sigh* But the beds here are so nice!

t_ch: 收到姬子的信息了吧？该回去了。若是延误了发车，列车长又该唠叨了。
t_en: Did you get Himeko's message? It's time to go. The conductor will nag us again if we put the Express behind schedule.

t_ch: 收到了收到了——带好行李和纪念品，我们回家！
t_en: Yeah, yeah. Well, pack your souvenirs and stuff. Back home we go!

t_ch: 离开雅利洛-Ⅵ，回到列车。
t_en: Leave Jarilo-VI and return to the Express.

t_ch: 还想再看这个世界几眼…
t_en: I want to have one last look at this world...

t_ch: …「优先级」？什么意思？难道在你看来，下层区城镇的重建工作不够重要吗？
t_en: ...Priorities? What do you mean? Are you saying rebuilding the Underworld isn't one of your "priorities"?

t_ch: 不！当然不是这个意思——只是我们当下可调用的资源有限，必须重新规划。
t_en: Of course not! I'm just saying we have limited assets, and we need new parameters.

t_ch: 资源…规划…你现在怎么就爱往我脸上甩这种词？
t_en: Assets... parameters... You sure love your fancy words.

t_ch: 算了，这事你别管了。看来还得我自己下去…啊——
t_en: Ugh, forget it. Leave it to me. I'll go down myself...

t_ch: …不要意气用事，希儿！我不会放你——
t_en: ...Come on, Seele! I won't leave you to—

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，看！
t_en: Look, Bronya!

t_ch: 他们这一路上…肯定还会遇到不少困难吧？
t_en: There'll be plenty of difficulties waiting for them, right?

t_ch: 嗯…也许会比我们面临的未来更加艰险。
t_en: Yes... their future is filled with even more uncertainty than ours.

t_ch: 但我们一定会战胜困难。哪怕这意味着，类似这样的争执要一直持续下去……
t_en: But we will prevail here... Even if it means arguments like this go on forever...

t_ch: …嘿，吵架我可是很在行的。
t_en: ...Hmph, they won't go on forever if I win them.

t_ch: 到啦，永冬铭碑！拍照拍照！
t_en: Here we are, Everwinter Monument! Photo time!

t_ch: 咦，佩拉和虎克也在？怎么说，要不要把她俩也拍进去？
t_en: Oh, Pela and Hook are here too? Let's make sure we get them in the background!

t_ch: 不必了，我追求画面纯净。
t_en: Maybe we could try for a cleaner composition for this one...

t_ch: 好主意，拍完也给她们看看。
t_en: Good idea. Let's show them the photos once we're done.

t_ch: 嗯…接下来去哪儿呢……
t_en: Okay... Where to next?

t_ch: 对了，咱们回来以后还没见着希露瓦呢！去机械屋看看吧。
t_en: Oh, we haven't seen Serval since we got back! Let's head to the workshop.

t_ch: ——谁啊？不敲门就闯进来，知不知道……
t_en: Who's there? Didn't your parents tell you to knock before—

t_ch: 哎呀，原来是你们仨！快来快来，我跟克拉拉刚才还聊起你们了呢。
t_en: Ha, it's you three! Come on in! I was just talking to Clara about you.

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}——希露瓦姐姐的小屋真的好神奇，什么宝贝都有…克拉拉很喜欢这里！
t_en: How have you been, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}? Miss Serval's workshop is amazing — there are all kinds of things... I like it here a lot!

t_ch: 克拉拉这孩子…真是太懂事了！哪像那群筑城者家的熊孩子，张口就是「阿姨」、「阿姨」……
t_en: "Miss Serval..." Clara is such a well-mannered kid! Meanwhile, the Architects' children are still calling me "auntie" and making me feel old...

t_ch: 来拍张照留作纪念吧！
t_en: Let's take a photo together!

t_ch: 机械屋还招助手吗？
t_en: Still looking to hire assistants for the workshop?

t_ch: 不对不对，才不是这句台词！我们是来拍照留念的。
t_en: Hey, that's not your line! We're here to take a photo, remember?

t_ch: 拍照？我还以为只有粉丝见面会才有这个环节呢——看在你们仨的面子上，我可以帮你们免费签名哦！
t_en: Photo? ...This feels like one of my fan meet-ups, haha. Anyway, for you guys, I'd do autographs for free.

t_ch: 「拍照」…啊，克拉拉想起来了——我还小的时候，史瓦罗先生也给克拉拉「拍照」过……
t_en: "Photo..." Oh! I remember. When I was little, Mr. Svarog took a photo for me...

t_ch: 来，克拉拉！站过来点儿，姐姐教你几个拍照的最佳角度……
t_en: Over here, Clara! Wanna learn some cool poses?

t_ch: 拍完了吗？快，给我们看看！
t_en: Done? Let's have a look!

t_ch: 哎呀，真不错！我演唱会前排的摄影师要是有你一半的功力就好了！
t_en: Wow, that's pretty great! I wish the photographers at my shows had your skills!

t_ch: 咦…克拉拉原来这么矮吗？我还以为……
t_en: Huh...? Am I really that short? I thought...

t_ch: 没事没事，只要营养跟上了，克拉拉也一定能长到和丹恒一样高的！
t_en: Don't worry! If you eat like Dan Heng, you'll be as tall as him one day! *stifled laughter*

t_ch: …我可以假装没听见。
t_en: ...I'll let that slip.

t_ch: 这张照片就送给你们吧，就当作是列车组的临别礼物。
t_en: Here, keep it... a little farewell gift from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 这…你这完全就是拍了个寂寞嘛！
t_en: ...There's gotta be a better shot than this one!

t_ch: 唔…明明克拉拉费了很大力气才没有眨眼……
t_en: Um... I was trying really hard not to blink...

t_ch: 唉…咱就不该相信业余摄影师……
t_en: *sigh* We shouldn't have trusted an amateur...

t_ch: 呃，算了，怎么说也算有点纪念意义…这张照片就送给你们吧，就当作是列车组的临别礼物。
t_en: Never mind, at least it's memorable... A little farewell gift from the Astral Express.

t_ch: 啊…你们这么快就要走了吗？
t_en: Huh...? You're leaving so soon?

t_ch: 我明白了，那这张照片我可得好好保管。晚点我也会帮你复制一份的，克拉拉。
t_en: *sigh* I understand. Well, this photo will always be close to my heart. I'll make a copy for you too, Clara.

t_ch: 谢、谢谢希露瓦姐姐！{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，三月姐姐，还有丹恒哥哥…感谢你们此前的照顾，希望你们这一路上能平平安安的。
t_en: Th—Thanks Miss Serval! {F#Big sister}{M#Big brother} {NICKNAME}, March, Dan Heng... Thanks for taking care of me. Safe travels.

t_ch: 有时间的话…请一定要再来看克拉拉和史瓦罗先生呀。
t_en: If you have time... please come back and visit me and Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 呵…照片拍完了，街也逛累了，接下来……
t_en: *yawn* ... That's enough sightseeing and photo-taking to last me a while...

t_ch: …接下来，就该去歌德宾馆好好睡上一觉了！
t_en: ...Time to head back to the Goethe Hotel for a good night's sleep!

t_ch: …回列车再睡。
t_en: ...We can sleep when we're back on the Express.

t_ch: 什么啊，之前都住进豪华客房了，却被那个坏女人给算计了一把…你们不觉得不甘心吗？
t_en: Not so fast! Last time we were here, that evil Madam Guardian interrupted us before we'd gotten the most out of our luxury suites... Don't you think we should make up for it?

t_ch: 走啦走啦，才一晚而已——姬子和杨叔都是成熟的大人，很有耐心的！
t_en: Come onnnn! Himeko and Mr. Yang are grown-ups — I'm sure they'll cope without us for one night.

t_ch: 再说了，贝洛伯格难得变得这么热闹，你们就不想再多找人聊聊天吗？我反正还没待够呢！
t_en: Besides, don't you want to chit-chat with the locals now that Belobog is full of life again? I know I do!

t_ch: 终于到了这个时候…唉，道别真是令人伤感呀……
t_en: Here we are at last... *sigh* It's sad to say goodbye.

t_ch: 天下没有不散的宴席。等回到列车上，你的伤感就该消失得差不多了。
t_en: Nothing lasts forever. You'll stop feeling sad the moment you set foot on the Express.

t_ch: 在你眼里我就这么没心没肺吗……
t_en: Hey, I'm not that heartless y'know...

t_ch: 潮水般的倦意向你袭来……
t_en: You're overwhelmed by a sea of weariness...

t_ch: 喔…喔…！好大的冰块呀！但是这里明明很温暖…上层区人就不怕冰块化掉嘛？
t_en: Whoaaaa! So much ice! But it's warm here... aren't you Overworlders worried it'll melt?

t_ch: 不会哦。虽然颜色跟冰块很接近，但你们眼前看到的其实是用地髓结晶雕刻成的纪念碑。
t_en: Not at all. It may look like ice, but it's actually a monument carved out of Geomarrow crystal.

t_ch: 啊，地髓！可惜老爹不在，否则肯定能把它卖出个好价钱！
t_en: Ah, Geomarrow! It's a shame daddy isn't here — He'd find a buyer for it and sell it for big bucks!

t_ch: 呃…怎么说呢？只有这块地髓是绝对的「非卖品」……
t_en: Uh... How do I explain... This particular piece of Geomarrow is definitely not for sale...

t_ch: 看，我给你们拍了照片！
t_en: Have a look! I took a photo of everyone.

t_ch: 照片？那是啥，快给虎克看看！
t_en: A "photo?" What's that? Hook wants to see it!

t_ch: 我必须提醒你，未经他人同意就进行拍摄，是违反贝洛伯格治安……
t_en: I must remind you that taking photos of others without gaining their permission violates Belobog public security ordinance number...

t_ch: …欸？你的拍照技术…还挺不错的嘛。而且…画面居然这么清晰？
t_en: ...Wait, that's a nice picture! And... it's crystal clear.

t_ch: 哇，这张「照片」…太厉害啦！可以送给虎克吗？
t_en: Wow! Photos are awesome! ...Can Hook have it?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，喜欢吧？拿去拿去，就当是姐姐送你的临别礼物啦！
t_en: Hee-hee, you like it? It's yours. A farewell gift from us.

t_ch: 那个，请问…请问您是奥列格兵长吗？
t_en: U—Uh, excuse me... are you Captain Oleg?

t_ch: …哦？没想到啊，过去这么长时间，居然还有人记着我的名字呢？
t_en: ...Huh? I didn't think anyone would still remember my name after all this time.

t_ch: 当然听过！您从前的英勇战绩，每个年轻铁卫都耳熟能详！
t_en: Of course we remember! Every young guard knows about your courageous exploits!

t_ch: 是吗？那你们都听说什么了？是只用三发子弹就解决了失控徘徊者的那回，还是连续六天不吃不喝，死守被怪物围攻哨站的那回？
t_en: Oh? Which ones might they be? The time I finished off that rampaging prowler with just three bullets? Or when I defended the outpost from monsters for six days straight without food or water?

t_ch: 您说的这两件事，我都没听过…请、请务必仔细给我讲讲！
t_en: I haven't heard of either of them actually... But I'd love to hear all about them!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，好！那我也不去别处闲逛了，就趁这个机会跟你好好说道说道……
t_en: Hahaha, sure! In which case, I'll cut my walk short and tell you all the grisly details...

t_ch: 喔哦！克拉拉，你看到了嘛？刚才有一头超大——超大的铁皮怪兽跑过去啦！
t_en: Wow! Did you see that, Clara? A super suuuper big steel monster just ran past!

t_ch: 那应该不是怪兽…史瓦罗先生的资料里有相关的记录，它叫…唔 ，一下子想不起来了。
t_en: I don't think that's a monster... Mr. Svarog's database has information about it. It's called... hmm, I can't remember.

t_ch: 没关系，名字没那么重要啦！最关键的是，我看到了哦——那个怪兽，它的胃口超级大，把很多人都吞进去了！
t_en: Don't worry about the name! The important thing is I saw what it can do — it has a huuuuge appetite and swallows people whole!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，鼹鼠党的下个目标已经定啦…打倒铁皮怪物，解救被吃掉的倒霉蛋们！
t_en: And it just so happens to be The Moles' next target, hehe... We'll defeat the iron monster and save the people in its tummy!

t_ch: 唔…克拉拉觉得，事情应该不是这样的……
t_en: Um... I don't think that's how it works...

t_ch: 莎夏！我的莎夏，是你吗？
t_en: Sasha! My Sasha, is that you?

t_ch: 爸爸——琥珀王在上，我还以为自己这辈子都见不到你了！
t_en: Dad! — by the Amber Lord, I thought I was never going to see you again!

t_ch: 赞美存护，孩子，赞美祂的宽宏…这个小家伙是…？
t_en: Praise the Preservation, my child. Praise THEIR leniency... And who's this little one?

t_ch: 快，巴里…快叫爷爷！
t_en: Here, Barry... say hello to grandpa!

t_ch: …爷爷！
t_en: ...Hello grandpa!

t_ch: 我的孙子…这是我的小孙子？呜……
t_en: My grandson... Is this my grandson? *sob*

t_ch: 抱歉，爸爸，本来应该让你也见自己女婿一面的。但他在杂志社的工作实在太忙，脱不开身。
t_en: Sorry Dad, I wanted to introduce you to my husband too, but he can't get away from the publisher today.

t_ch: 没关系的，女儿，我们以后有的是机会…等会儿，你刚才说「杂志社」？
t_en: That's okay, my child, we've got all the time in the... did you say "publisher"?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你有过类似的感受吗？偶尔停下脚步，回想自己曾经做过的事…你会感叹自己一路以来居然战胜了那么多的困难，感叹自己曾经坚强得难以置信……
t_en: I don't know if you've ever had that feeling, {NICKNAME}... When you stop and look back on the things you've done, and wonder at all the obstacles you've overcome, the strength you've displayed...

t_ch: 然后你会陷入自我怀疑。怀疑你已经失去了当初的那份能量…怀疑如果一切再重来一次，你会无法复制那个勇敢的自己。
t_en: And then you start to doubt yourself, and wonder if you've lost that drive... If everything were to happen again, would you still stand strong?

t_ch: 我感同身受。
t_en: I know the feeling.

t_ch: 你能理解？太好了…我的意思是，知道这世界上有人能和自己感同身受，真是再好不过的事了。
t_en: You do? Good... I mean, it's good to know someone in this world feels the same way as me.

t_ch: 我还不太能理解你的感受。
t_en: I don't quite understand what you mean.

t_ch: 的确，你和大多数人都不一样——你全身上下，无时无刻不散发着自信。
t_en: And that's not surprising — you're different from others. You seem to radiate a confidence most people don't have.

t_ch: 封锁的这十几年里，我的精神没有一秒不处于紧绷的状态。倘若我有丝毫松懈，「地火」的根基就有可能动摇…下层区就有可能陷入万劫不复的境地。
t_en: I haven't been able to sit back once over the past ten years — a moment's negligence could have brought Wildfire crashing down... and the Underworld would've been beyond saving.

t_ch: 最难的不是时刻保持警醒，而是看不到坚持的尽头…不知道自己何时才能放松下来。
t_en: Staying vigilant wasn't the issue... it was the not knowing whether I'd ever be able to let my guard down again...

t_ch: 不管别人怎么想——在我心里，你们的到来就是对我漫长守候的奖赏。谢谢你，「开拓者」。
t_en: No matter what anyone says, I believe your arrival was the reward for my long vigil. Thank you, Trailblazer.

t_ch: 假如一直低着头行走，每一步都害怕陷进泥潭…我们的脚步一定会越发沉重，士气也会变得低迷。
t_en: If you walk with your head down and your heart full of fear, your steps will grow heavier and your spirit will suffer.

t_ch: 如果我们找到了某样值得仰望的东西…满怀希望地上路，步子都会变得轻盈，就算被绊倒了也能很快振作起来，重新出发。
t_en: But if you find something to look up to and move forward with hope, then your steps will grow lighter... and if you should fall, you'll be on your feet before you know it.

t_ch: ——谢谢你们教会了我这些，开拓者。
t_en: ...Thank you for teaching me this, Trailblazers.

t_ch: 上任仪式进行得很顺利。我是没想到，居然会有那么多下层区居民跑来听宣讲。
t_en: The inauguration ceremony went very smoothly. I didn't expect so many Underworlders to come and listen to the address.

t_ch: 而且大家也都表现出了支持。看来布洛妮娅给他们留下的印象还不错。
t_en: Everyone seemed supportive, too. Seems like Bronya made a pretty good impression on them.

t_ch: 兴许是两位首领美言了几句。
t_en: Maybe Natasha and Oleg helped promote her a little.

t_ch: 的确有可能，他们一向想得周到。
t_en: Possibly. They always think ahead.

t_ch: 只要他们俩都认可布洛妮娅的为人，那追随「地火」的大家也就不会质疑了。
t_en: If those two approve of Bronya, then everyone following Wildfire won't question her either.

t_ch: 你在现场吗？我没看到你。
t_en: Were you there? I don't think I saw you.

t_ch: 当然在，我只是混在人群里。你们没注意到我，就证明我的目标达到了——我想用自己的方式保证布洛妮娅的安全。
t_en: Of course I was! I was just hidden in the crowd. I'm glad you didn't notice me — means I'm doing something right. I want to protect Bronya in my own way.

t_ch: 她原本希望我能站在她身边，但我觉得这样更好。更何况…我也不太习惯被那么多双眼睛盯着。
t_en: She wanted me to stand next to her, but I'm glad I stuck to my guns. Besides, I'm not sure I could handle that many people staring at me.

t_ch: 她会成为一位杰出的领袖。
t_en: She will be an outstanding leader.

t_ch: 那是当然。还在下层区的时候，我就看出来了。
t_en: No doubt. I figured that out back in the Underworld.

t_ch: 我们马上要离开贝洛伯格了。
t_en: We're about to leave Belobog.

t_ch: 离开…？这么快？我以为你们还能再多待几天，至少等到情况稳定下来……
t_en: Leave...? So soon? I thought you'd stay for a few more days — at least until everything had calmed down...

t_ch: 啊…我明白了。还有人在等着你们回去，然后才好继续旅行，对吧？
t_en: Ahh... I get it. People are waiting for you to go back so you can keep traveling, right?

t_ch: 可惜，我本来以为咱们还有时间…还能好好聊聊那些我丁点儿都不懂的话题。看来只有等下次了。
t_en: Shame, I thought we'd have more time to discuss all the crazy things you guys like to talk about. Looks like that'll have to wait till next time.

t_ch: …可别否定我。我坚信，咱们一定会再见面的。
t_en: ...And before you say anything, there will be a next time. I know we'll meet again.

t_ch: 上任仪式开始之前，我收到了布洛妮娅的消息——她把在贝洛伯格北方发生的事都告诉我了。关于「星核」，还有可可利亚的牺牲……
t_en: I received Bronya's message before the inauguration ceremony. She told me everything that happened north of Belobog — about the Stellaron, and Cocolia's sacrifice...

t_ch: 不过……
t_en: But...

t_ch: …不过？
t_en: ...But?

t_ch: 呃…听布洛妮娅的就对了。
t_en: Uhh... Just listen to Bronya.

t_ch: 老实说，我觉得那姑娘在对我掩饰什么。以我对可可利亚的了解…她才不是那种会突然转变心意的家伙。
t_en: ...Honestly, I feel that girl is hiding something from me. I knew Cocolia well, and she was never one to suddenly change her mind.

t_ch: 不过放心吧，我不会深究的。布洛妮娅既然选择了这套说辞，证明她肯定已经充分考虑过了。
t_en: *sigh* Don't worry, I won't dig too deep. I know Bronya must have thought long and hard before deciding what to make public.

t_ch: 只是对我来说，缺少了「星核」这个曾经疯狂追逐的课题…心里难免有点空落落的。
t_en: I just... feel a bit empty now that we've lost the Stellaron — I spent a long time pursuing it, after all...

t_ch: 至于可可利亚…至少她能以伟人的身份被大多数人记住。这样也好……
t_en: As for Cocolia, the best side of her will live on in people's memory. That's not all bad...

t_ch: 希露瓦姐姐…很酷，很厉害，而且还很开朗……
t_en: Miss Serval is cool, strong, and... carefree...

t_ch: 克拉拉一定也要成为像她一样的大人，这样才能保护好大家——也能保护史瓦罗先生！
t_en: I want to be like her when I grow up so I can protect everyone... and protect Mr. Svarog!

t_ch: 这一晃就是十好几年…真高兴还能在宾馆大堂见到你，大哥。
t_en: It's been more than a decade... I'm so glad to see you in the hotel lobby again, Mr. Goethe.

t_ch: 不可思议…上次带你参观宾馆就好像是昨天发生的事。我年纪大了，很多事都记不清了…但只有这件事的画面一天比一天清晰。
t_en: Unbelievable... feels like it was just yesterday that I was showing you around the hotel all those years ago. I'm too old to remember a lot of things... but that day I remember clearly as ever.

t_ch: 既然上下层的联系又被打通了，咱们两家也该多交流交流待客经验了。
t_en: Since the passage between the Overworld and Underworld has opened up again, it's about time we shared some tips with each other, hotel manager to hotel manager.

t_ch: 当然，当然！我之前还一直担心，没了上层区的客源，老妹你的生意会不会做不下去…没成想你的饭店还活的好好的，多亏了你经营有方！
t_en: Of course, of course! I was worried before that after losing customers from the Overworld you would go out of business... but your establishment is still alive and well, all thanks to your superb management!

t_ch: 过奖了，歌德大哥！我家店面正好在装修，等我忙活完了，一定请你再来参观一次。
t_en: You flatter me, Goethe! My hotel is getting refurbished. I'll be sure to invite you to visit again when the work is all done.

t_ch: 这些积灰的文件…
t_en: These dusty papers...

t_ch: 都需要大守护者签署。
t_en: All requires the signature of the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 居然堆积了这么多？
t_en: How could there be so many?

t_ch: 可可利亚大人究竟…
t_en: But Madam Cocolia...

t_ch: 上一任大守护者…
t_en: The previous Supreme Guardian...

t_ch: 禁止我们在这里办公。
t_en: ...forbid us to work here.

t_ch: 那姑娘从小疑神疑鬼…
t_en: That girl was always paranoid since she was little...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅就开朗得多。
t_en: Bronya is far more optimistic.

t_ch: 呃，你是下层区人？
t_en: Oh, you're from the Underworld?

t_ch: 对，土生土长的。
t_en: That's right, born and raised.

t_ch: 有什么问题吗？
t_en: Is something wrong?

t_ch: 没什么，就是诧异…
t_en: No, it's just surprising...

t_ch: 毕竟你的谈吐很端庄。
t_en: After all, you are very dignified in your speech.

t_ch: 好宽敞的大堂啊…
t_en: What a spacious lobby...

t_ch: 妈妈小时住过这里哦。
t_en: My mother lived here as a child, you know.

t_ch: 那，阿丽娜也能住吗？
t_en: So can Alina stay here too?

t_ch: 嗯…让妈妈看看价格…
t_en: Yes, let me check with mother about the price...

t_ch: …歌蒂？是歌蒂，对吧？
t_en: ...Gertie? It's Gertie, right?

t_ch: 好久不见，歌德大哥！
t_en: Mr. Goethe! Long time no see!

t_ch: 人呢…怎么还没到啊……
t_en: ...Where did they get to?

t_ch: ——哟，真是说什么来什么！我正想着你什么时候会到呢，嘿。
t_en: Ah, speak of the devil! I was just wondering when you might show up, hehe.

t_ch: 毕竟有免费的下午茶…
t_en: I never say no to a free afternoon tea...

t_ch: 放心，饮料甜点都管够！今天我请客，想喝什么随便点。
t_en: Don't worry, there's enough to go around! On the house — order whatever you like.

t_ch: 这么着急啊…我都没准备呢。
t_en: Didn't realize you were in such a hurry... I'm not ready yet.

t_ch: 咱俩谁跟谁呀，有什么好准备的～敞开肚子喝就是啦。
t_en: Ready? My stomach's always ready for food and drink!

t_ch: 让你帮我点的东西呢？
t_en: Have you placed my orders yet?

t_ch: …啊？什么东西？
t_en: ...Huh? What orders?

t_ch: 噢，原来我没开消息提醒……
t_en: Oops, forgot to check my messages...

t_ch: 抱歉抱歉，是我不好…不过现在点也来得及，想喝什么随便点！
t_en: Sorry, my bad... but it's not too late! You can order anything you want!

t_ch: 毕竟…今天可能要找你咨询挺多事的，只好用这种方法弥补啦。
t_en: There's something I need your help with — I had to make it up to you somehow.

t_ch: 不过，今天来喝下午茶的人可真够多的。恐怕我们俩得站着聊了。
t_en: ...Looks like we're not the only ones who wanted afternoon tea today though. We'll have to stand up for the time being.

t_ch: 那我可要敞开喝了。
t_en: Well, I'll help myself to the tea then.

t_ch: 哈哈，也别喝太多啦，小心晚上睡不着。
t_en: Haha, don't drink too much, or you won't be able to sleep tonight.

t_ch: 不过嘛，话说回来…其实我约你这趟出来，是有点事想拜托你。
t_en: Anyway... about the something I needed your help with.

t_ch: 有事直说呗，咱俩谁跟谁呀。
t_en: No need for the formalities. Let's hear it!

t_ch: 不愧是见过大世面的人，真大方！弄得我怪不好意思的……
t_en: Well, you're clearly better traveled than I am, and I know how generous you are... I feel embarrassed to ask now...

t_ch: 行吧，看在你请客的份上…
t_en: You have my attention... as long as the afternoon tea keeps flowing.

t_ch: 你先别急着答应啊，这事可能比你想的要复杂……
t_en: Ha, don't get ahead of yourself, this might be more complicated than you think...

t_ch: 就知道有诈！我先溜了…
t_en: Already sounds dangerous — bye!

t_ch: 欸，别跑！别紧张呀，我只是想问你点事……
t_en: Wait, don't go! Take it easy, I just wanted to ask you a question...

t_ch: …你是不是有事求我？
t_en: ...This "something..." is it a favor?

t_ch: 呃，我的意图就这么明显吗？大意了呀……
t_en: *sigh* Was it that obvious? I'm out of practice...

t_ch: 有事直说呗，咱俩谁跟谁呀。
t_en: No need for the formalities. Let's hear it!

t_ch: 不愧是见过大世面的人，真大方！弄得我怪不好意思的……
t_en: Well, you're clearly better traveled than I am, and I know how generous you are... I feel embarrassed to ask now...

t_ch: 行吧，看在你请客的份上…
t_en: You have my attention... as long as the afternoon tea keeps flowing.

t_ch: 你先别急着答应啊，这事可能比你想的要复杂……
t_en: Ha, don't get ahead of yourself, this might be more complicated than you think...

t_ch: 「打扫卫生」出卖了你。
t_en: This is the price you pay for cleaning.

t_ch: …你怎么还记得这茬呀？
t_en: Uh... You still remember that?

t_ch: 哎，让我想想该怎么开口……
t_en: Uh, how can I put this...

t_ch: 没记错的话，你、三月还有丹恒，你们是坐那个什么——对了，「星穹列车」——来到贝洛伯格的，对吧？
t_en: If I remember correctly, you, March, and Dan Heng came here on that train — the "Astral Express" — right?

t_ch: 其实，我是想问问你…你们这趟列车，还欢迎新乘客吗？
t_en: I was wondering... is the train accepting new passengers?

t_ch: 怎么突然问这个？
t_en: Why are you asking this all of a sudden?

t_ch: 嗐，这不是…尘埃落定了嘛。星核的问题也解决了，可可利亚她也……
t_en: Well, i—it's just... the dust has settled now. The Stellaron problem has been resolved, and Cocolia...

t_ch: …哎，算了算了，没什么！刚才那句话你当我没说。
t_en: ...Forget it, it's nothing! Forget I said anything.

t_ch: 呃…需要通过上车测验才行。
t_en: Um... You need to pass the Boarding Exam first.

t_ch: 上车测验…是考试的意思？这个我在行！原来你们的规矩跟贝洛伯格的学校也差不多嘛。
t_en: Boarding Exam? Like a test? I can do that! That sounds like the admission system that schools in Belobog use!

t_ch: 我就在想，你们会不会禁止——呃…我这种「外星人」——上车？
t_en: I'm wondering though, are there any restrictions against, uh... "aliens" like me from boarding the train?

t_ch: 这我说了不算，得帮你问问。
t_en: Such matters aren't up to me, I'm afraid I'll have to ask around first.

t_ch: 嗐…我想也是，哈哈。
t_en: ...I guessed as much, hehe.

t_ch: 你看，我留在这儿也没什么事可做。无非就是继续待在「机械屋」，修修设备、练练琴…演出的氛围是不错，不过听众来来回回也就那么几个。
t_en: You see, there's not much left for me here except to carry on repairing equipment in the workshop... I like practicing the guitar, and performances are fun, but the crowds are pretty small.

t_ch: 再说了，我可是搞科研出身…探索新世界的大好机会就摆在眼前，怎么能不心动？贝洛伯格这小地方，任谁住上个把年也会觉得腻了吧？
t_en: Besides, I'm a researcher... how could I not get excited about the chance to explore new worlds up close? Everyone gets tired of living in small places like Belobog after a few years, right?

t_ch: 我理解你。欢迎上车！
t_en: I understand how you feel. Welcome aboard!

t_ch: 真的吗，你就这么答应了？
t_en: Really? You're gonna say yes just like that?

t_ch: …但还是得先问问别人意见。
t_en: ...But I'll still have to ask the others for their input first.

t_ch: 理解理解。那这件事就拜托你啦。
t_en: I understand. I'm counting on you!

t_ch: 我可以帮你咨询一下专家意见…
t_en: I can consult an expert opinion on your behalf...

t_ch: 行啊行啊，那这件事就拜托你了！
t_en: Okay, okay, I—I'm counting on you!

t_ch: 我也知道，这个要求实在太突然、太任性了…先提前给你道个歉啦。
t_en: I know this is a lot to ask out of the blue, so apologies in advance.

t_ch: 没问题，包在我身上。
t_en: Not a problem, just leave it to me.

t_ch: 哈～托你办事，我能放一百个心！
t_en: Haha, I knew I could count on you!

t_ch: 歌德先生，麻烦再加一碟曲奇——
t_en: Mr. Goethe, one plate of cookies, please...

t_ch: 不用这么郑重…这不像你。
t_en: Not like you to be so serious...

t_ch: 哎，我这不是有求于你嘛～
t_en: Hehe, this is a pretty big ask⁓

t_ch: 歌德先生，麻烦再加一碟曲奇——
t_en: Mr. Goethe, one plate of cookies, please...

t_ch: 比起道歉，我更喜欢行动…
t_en: Action speaks louder than words...

t_ch: 行动？哦～懂了，我等会儿再给你加碟小零食吧！
t_en: ...Actions? Ohhhh, I get you! Another plate of delicious snacks, coming right up!

t_ch: 歌德先生，麻烦再加一碟曲奇——
t_en: Mr. Goethe, one plate of cookies, please...

t_ch: （希露瓦回去了…发短信找姬子小姐问问希露瓦的请求吧。）
t_en: (There goes Serval... I better send Himeko a text and ask about the request...)

t_ch: （…大概也可以问问杨叔和帕姆？）
t_en: (...or I guess I could ask Mr. Yang — or Pom-Pom?)

t_ch: （那么，该向谁请教好呢……）
t_en: (Hmm, who's the best one to ask...?)

t_ch: 姬子小姐。
t_en: Himeko.

t_ch: （发个短信给姬子小姐吧……）
t_en: (Let's send a message to Himeko...)

t_ch: 问问瓦尔特先生。
t_en: Mr. Yang.

t_ch: （发个短信给瓦尔特先生吧……）
t_en: (Let's send a message to Mr. Yang...)

t_ch: 问问列车长帕姆。
t_en: The conductor — Pom-Pom.

t_ch: （发个短信给帕姆吧……）
t_en: (Let's send a message to Pom-Pom...)

t_ch: 这是布洛妮娅大人的请求……
t_en: This is Lady Bronya's request...

t_ch: 姐姐，你就去和布洛妮娅大人聊聊吧。这毕竟是她亲自发出的邀请……
t_en: You should go and talk to Lady Bronya, Serval. It was her personal invite, after all...

t_ch: 唉…知道啦知道啦，你让我考虑一下……
t_en: Ugh... I know, I know. Let me think about it...

t_ch: 你们在聊什么？
t_en: What are you guys chatting about?

t_ch: 希露瓦，我问过他们了——
t_en: Serval, I already asked them...

t_ch: 哦？{NICKNAME}你来得正好！已经有消息了吗？
t_en: Huh? {NICKNAME}, you're just in time! Any news yet?

t_ch: 我是不是来得不是时候？
t_en: Is this a bad time?

t_ch: 欸？没这回事，你来得正好！快快快，来帮我解个围……
t_en: Oh! No, not at all! I could do with a hand, actually...

t_ch: 嗯？{NICKNAME}，你来得正好——请你也帮忙劝劝她吧。
t_en: Perfect timing, {NICKNAME} — please talk some sense into her.

t_ch: 我就知道……
t_en: *sigh* I knew it...

t_ch: 文劝还是武劝？
t_en: So, am I supposed to be the good cop here?

t_ch: …你到底是帮哪边的啊？！
t_en: ...Hey, you're taking his side already!?

t_ch: 你们的家务事，我不想掺和…
t_en: I try to stay out of family squabbles...

t_ch: 太对了——这事让我们姐弟俩自己解决就行……
t_en: Y'know what, you're right — we should work these things out on our own...

t_ch: 我还没搞清楚情况，我不站边。
t_en: I'm not taking sides — I don't even know what the deal is.

t_ch: 哪有什么情况……
t_en: That's just it — there is no deal...

t_ch: …希露瓦，我理解你不愿意再重回铁卫编制的想法。
t_en: ...Serval, I understand your reluctance to rejoin the Silvermane Guards.

t_ch: 但布洛妮娅大人很看重你的才能，她觉得你能为贝洛伯格的复兴贡献许多力量…所以我来找你聊这件事。
t_en: But Lady Bronya values your talent, and she sees how much you could contribute to the rejuvenation of Belobog... so I came to talk to you about it.

t_ch: 不管怎么说，你至少该去见她一面吧。
t_en: In any case, you should at least pay her a visit.

t_ch: 好吧…我明白了。那我就跑克里珀堡一趟，把真实想法全都告诉布洛妮娅。
t_en: Okay... I get it. I'll make a trip to Qlipoth Fort and tell Bronya what I really think.

t_ch: 不好意思啦，{NICKNAME}——我先失陪一会儿。等我回来以后，再把你那边的情况给我讲讲吧。
t_en: Sorry, {NICKNAME} — I need to get this over with. Let me know what they said when I get back.

t_ch: 呵…姐姐她一直是这种性格。
t_en: *sigh* ...Same old Serval.

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 啊，抱歉——刚才只顾着和希露瓦说话，忘了向你解释事情的原委。
t_en: Ah, sorry, my head's still in the argument. It's like this.

t_ch: 毕竟那是希露瓦嘛。
t_en: That's your sister for you.

t_ch: 听你的意思，她也给你摆出过难题？
t_en: Is she creating trouble for you too?

t_ch: 啊，抱歉——刚才只顾着和姐姐说话，忘了向你解释事情的原委。
t_en: Ah, sorry, my head's still in the argument. It's like this.

t_ch: 她想走这事惊动布洛妮娅了？
t_en: Was Bronya caught off guard by her desire to join the Astral Express?

t_ch: 上车？
t_en: Join the Express?

t_ch: 原来如此…怪不得希露瓦的神情那么慌张。原来她心里是这么盘算的……
t_en: I see... no wonder she seemed so flustered. So that's what's on her mind...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉——刚才只顾着和姐姐说话，忘了向你解释事情的原委。
t_en: Ah, sorry, my head's still in the argument. It's like this.

t_ch: 你应该知道，姐姐曾经是银鬃铁卫技术部的研究组长之一——「星核」是她的主攻课题。
t_en: As you know, my sister used to be one of the research team leads in the Silvermane Guards Technology Division. Stellarons were her main area of research.

t_ch: 但希露瓦的专精远不止于对「星核」的研究，她在机械工程等其它领域也有很深的造诣。
t_en: But her expertise went far beyond Stellarons. She was and still is well versed in mechanical engineering and other fields.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅大人继任之后，立刻就想到了姐姐的才能。大守护者希望召回她，让她重新拾起技术研究工作，为振兴贝洛伯格的事业发光发热。
t_en: Immediately after her succession, Lady Bronya set her sights on Serval's talents. The supreme guardian wants her to resume her technological research and be a shining light for the revitalization of Belobog.

t_ch: 不过，你也看到她的态度了…我怕她此行前去也聊不出什么令人满意的结果，指不定还会闹出些不愉快。
t_en: However... you saw her attitude just now. I doubt her meeting with Lady Bronya will change anything — it'll probably just leave them both feeling dejected.

t_ch: 也许她只是想看看外面的世界。
t_en: Maybe she just wants to see the outside world.

t_ch: 我也受不了坐办公室的工作…
t_en: I couldn't stand an office job either...

t_ch: 嗯…个中原因或许没有那么简单。
t_en: Mm... I think there's more than one element to it.

t_ch: 直觉告诉我，这多半与可可利亚大人的离开有关。
t_en: My intuition tells me this is mostly linked to Madam Cocolia's passing.

t_ch: 她们不是早就绝交了？
t_en: I thought they became enemies a long time ago?

t_ch: 是的，但……
t_en: That's true, but...

t_ch: 听起来好像很有故事…细说。
t_en: Sounds like you've got a story to tell. Let's hear it.

t_ch: …算了，这也不过是我个人的猜测，我一向不擅长揣测姐姐的心思。
t_en: *sigh* Never mind, it's just a personal hunch. I've never been good at reading my sister's mind.

t_ch: 我看你们之间的交情不错，有机会的话，还是让她自己说给你听吧。
t_en: I can tell the two of you have a close bond. If you get the chance, see if you can persuade her to open up about it.

t_ch: 不过，老实说…我也不想看她成天坐在克里珀堡里，对着办公桌唉声叹气。
t_en: ...Hand on heart, I wouldn't want to see her sitting behind a desk in Qlipoth Fort and sighing all day either...

t_ch: 以姐姐的性格，跟着你们一起旅行或许才更合适。和她的求知欲、好奇心相比，贝洛伯格实在太狭小了。
t_en: Belobog is too small a place for a mind as inquisitive as Serval's... Maybe it would be better for her to join you on your journey.

t_ch: 啊…果然，我还是很在意她是怎么和布洛妮娅大人沟通的。
t_en: *sigh* ...I'll admit, I'm a little worried about how she plans on getting all this across to Lady Bronya.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，有空吗？我们一起去克里珀堡门口等她吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you busy? Let's go and wait for her in front of Qlipoth Fort.

t_ch: 她们似乎还没谈完。希望不会发生争执…我们还是进去看看吧。
t_en: Doesn't seem like they've finished talking yet. I hope it's not turning into a shouting match... Let's go in.

t_ch: …你的情况我了解了，希露瓦。我尊重你的选择。
t_en: ...I understand your circumstances, Serval. I respect your choice.

t_ch: 这次无端提出请求，没有将你自己的意愿纳入考量，的确是我的失误。
t_en: It was my mistake to make an unwarranted request without considering your own wishes.

t_ch: 我必须向你道歉…也希望你不要把这件事放在心上。
t_en: Allow me to offer you my apologies... I hope you don't take this matter to heart.

t_ch: 别、别这样！布洛妮娅，你现在可是大守护者了，不用因为这点小事……
t_en: N—No need, Bronya! You're a supreme guardian now. Don't let a little thing like this...

t_ch: 再说了，因为个人原因不能参与贝洛伯格的重建计划…该道歉的应该是我才对。
t_en: Besides, I'm the one who should be apologizing — I've put my own personal reasons ahead of Belobog's reconstruction...

t_ch: 我理解，希露瓦。毕竟你和母亲……
t_en: I get it, Serval. After all, you and mother...

t_ch: …抱歉，我不是想故意揭你的伤疤——
t_en: ...Sorry, I don't mean to open up old wounds...

t_ch: ——但有件事…我必须亲自和你确认。
t_en: ...but there's something I need to ask you.

t_ch: 这把吉他…是我在母亲大人的遗物中发现的。
t_en: This guitar... I found it after going through mother's old things.

t_ch: 她生前并不爱好音乐。我猜想它或许和你有联系……
t_en: She was never a music lover, so... I thought this might be connected to you...

t_ch: 可可利亚，你……
t_en: Cocolia, you...

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: …不，没什么。这的确是我的东西。
t_en: ...I—It's nothing. You're right, it does belong to me.

t_ch: …确切地说，它曾经属于我和可可利亚两个人，是我们共有的财产。你不介意的话，布洛妮娅…我能把它带回机械屋吗？
t_en: ...In fact, it once belonged to me and Cocolia. It was our shared treasure. If you don't mind, Bronya... could I take it back to the workshop?

t_ch: …当然。我猜这乐器的背后大概有一段故事…但这涉及你的隐私，我就不多过问了。
t_en: ...Of course. I—I'm sure there's a story behind it, but that's between you and mother — I won't pry.

t_ch: 希望你能有一段精彩的旅途，希露瓦。有机会的话，也一定记得回家看看。
t_en: I hope you have an amazing journey, Serval. Do remember to visit home when you get the chance.

t_ch: …我会的。谢谢你，布洛妮娅。
t_en: ...I will. Thank you, Bronya.

t_ch: 我和布洛妮娅的对话，你们都听到了？
t_en: I assume you heard our conversation just now?

t_ch: 我是没想到，她居然表现得那么豁达…也没说什么挽留的话。她该不会只是想找个理由召见我，然后把这吉他还回来吧？
t_en: I didn't expect her to be so open-minded... and she didn't even try to persuade me to stay. She didn't summon me just to return the guitar, did she?

t_ch: 也不排除这个可能。布洛妮娅大人善读人心…她应该知道，要是没有足够强烈的理由，你是绝对不会踏进克里珀堡半步的。
t_en: I wouldn't rule it out — she's good at reading people's minds... she would've known that you'd never set foot in Qlipoth Fort voluntarily.

t_ch: …没想到隔了这么多年，还能再见到这把吉他。真是一场孽缘…嗐。
t_en: ...After all these years, I never thought I'd set eyes on this guitar again. *sigh* Guess we're stuck with each other...

t_ch: 这吉他是个什么来头？
t_en: So what's the story behind it?

t_ch: …那可说来话长了。
t_en: ...Well, it'll take a while to tell.

t_ch: 可可利亚也喜欢摇滚吗？
t_en: Did Cocolia like rock 'n' roll too?

t_ch: 据我所知，前任大守护者没有类似的爱好。
t_en: If she did, I never knew about it.

t_ch: 这里面的故事…说来话长呀。
t_en: The story behind this guitar... huh, it'll take a while to tell.

t_ch: 还在士官学校上学的时候，我和可可利亚可是出了名的爱折腾。我俩总会整些莫名其妙的发明…美其名曰「探索新技术的无限可能性」。
t_en: When we were in cadet school, Cocolia and I were notoriously disruptive — we were always coming up with the next crazy invention... We called it "exploring the infinite possibilities of new technologies."

t_ch: 这把吉他…就是我们那时捣鼓出来的作品之一。可可利亚给它起的名字是——
t_en: This guitar was one of those inventions. Cocolia came up with a name...

t_ch: …「超电磁流射演奏器」，简称「电吉他」。
t_en: ...the "super electromagnetic flux player," or "electric guitar" for short.

t_ch: 原来她也是技术人员出身。
t_en: So she was tech-savvy too?

t_ch: 那倒不是，她读的是军事史专业，和我八竿子都打不着。
t_en: Not really, no. She was a military history major — very different from what I was doing.

t_ch: 不过嘛，天天跟我一起混…她会对这类发明产生兴趣也是再正常不过。
t_en: Still, we hung out every day, it's only natural that my love for inventing would rub off on her.

t_ch: 这名字…真不是你起的？
t_en: Wait, you sure you didn't come up with that name?

t_ch: …怎么，不信？想不到吧，可可利亚那家伙曾经也是很热血的。
t_en: Don't believe me? Let me tell you, Cocolia and I shared a similar way of thinking back in the day.

t_ch: 虽然现在听起来又长又傻，但当时的我还挺喜欢这名字的…因为很「摇滚」。
t_en: It's a bit of a mouthful, but I liked it at the time... it was a rock 'n' roll name.

t_ch: …哎，这个话题再聊下去那可就长了，咱回机械屋再接着聊。顺便…你们俩来陪我收拾行李吧？
t_en: ...*sigh*, I told you it'd take a while to tell the story — let's get back to the workshop first. By the way... wanna help me pack my bags?

t_ch: …咦？这个旋律是？
t_en: ...Huh? That melody...

t_ch: 我没记错的话…这是你写的曲子吧？
t_en: ...That's one of yours, right?

t_ch: 我还以为绝大多数人接受不了这风格呢——看来，我还是给这座城市留下了点记忆的嘛。
t_en: I didn't think many people liked that one... Seems the city might have a few nice memories of me after I'm gone.

t_ch: 好了，时候不早咯——我们回去吧。
t_en: Well, it's getting late, let's head back.

t_ch: 啊…还是家里舒服呀，在外面总觉得浑身不自在。
t_en: Ah... There's no place like home. I never feel completely free out there.

t_ch: 杰帕德——能给姐姐搭把手吗？帮我收拾收拾行李～
t_en: Gepard, can you lend your dear sister a hand? Help me pack my bags~

t_ch: 我能拒绝吗？你这屋子…光是看看都觉得头疼。
t_en: Am I allowed to refuse? This place... Just looking around gives me a headache.

t_ch: 好啦好啦，我保证这是最后一次。
t_en: Okay, okay, I promise this is the last time.

t_ch: 谁知道我跟着{NICKNAME}上车之后，什么时候才能回一趟家呢，你说对吧？你以后就是想帮我收拾行李，恐怕也没机会咯。
t_en: I'm about to go traveling with {NICKNAME} — who knows how long it'll be before I can visit home again. You should help me pack while you still have the chance.

t_ch: …知道了。我来帮你。
t_en: ...Fine. I'll give you a hand.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你就在这里随便看看，休息一下吧。也辛苦你啦！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you just need to make yourself at home. You've done plenty of work already!

t_ch: 希露瓦，这个植物标本是什么？
t_en: Serval, what kind of plant is this?

t_ch: 哦，那个啊～那是我们家最可爱的玲宝刚出门科考时寄回家的植物标本。
t_en: Oh, that~ It's a botanical specimen — little Lynxy sent it during one of her research trips.

t_ch: 不过这植物是叫什么来着？我有点忘了……
t_en: As for the name... I can't quite recall...

t_ch: …雪人掌。
t_en: ...Snowman's palm.

t_ch: 顺便一提，这位…「玲宝」，是朗道家年纪最小的妹妹。
t_en: And "little Lynxy" is the youngest sister of the Landau family, by the way...

t_ch: 哦，对对对——雪人掌！瞧我这记性。
t_en: Ah! Yes, yes, — snowman's palm! Ugh, my memory is the worst.

t_ch: 这可能是你家里唯一一盆看起来还活着的植物。
t_en: It's the only plant here that looks like it's still alive...

t_ch: …你还好意思说我！
t_en: Hey! Watch it.

t_ch: 嗐。不管怎么样，这件标本是很有纪念意义的，我也要带走。{NICKNAME}，谢谢你提醒。
t_en: ...Anyway, this specimen means a lot to me, so I'm bringing it with me. Thanks for the reminder, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 希露瓦，这桌上的是什么？
t_en: Serval, what's this on the desk?

t_ch: 哦，这个——这是我准备寄给老妈的信，还没写完呢。
t_en: Oh, that's a letter I was gonna send to my mom. I haven't even finished writing it.

t_ch: 倒也没写什么很私人的内容，你想看就看吧。
t_en: There's nothing personal in it — have a read if you like.

t_ch: 原来你还有在给爸妈写信啊……
t_en: You're still writing to Mom and Dad...?

t_ch: 只给老妈写。你也知道，我跟老爸不对付。
t_en: Only to Mom. You know I still don't get along with Dad.

t_ch: 姐姐，你如果真要离开贝洛伯格的话…走之前还是回趟家看看吧。
t_en: Y'know Serval, if you're really leaving Belobog for a while... you should pay them a visit first.

t_ch: …知道了，我会考虑考虑的。
t_en: ...Mm. I'll think about it.

t_ch: 现在能聊聊这把吉他了吗？
t_en: Serval, can we hear more about this guitar now?

t_ch: 既然你这么好奇，那我就介绍一下。
t_en: Since you're so curious, I'll give you the whole story.

t_ch: 这把吉他…其实是很久之前，可可利亚送给我的生日礼物。
t_en: This guitar... was actually given to me as a birthday present by Cocolia a long time ago.

t_ch: 图纸是我负责设计的，但由于部分原材料昂贵又稀有，我始终没能把它打造出来。是她帮我把概念变为了现实。
t_en: I came up with the design, but I was never able to build it — some of the raw materials were either too expensive or too hard to get a hold of. She helped me turn the concept into reality.

t_ch: 雷厉风行，说到做到——她的办事风格向来如此。那个时候，我还以为…我还以为我们会是一辈子的朋友。
t_en: Quick as lightning, always keeping her word — that was her way of doing things. Back then... I thought we'd be friends for life.

t_ch: 既然是礼物…为什么它会被放在可可利亚大人的办公室里？
t_en: If it was a gift... why was it in Madam Cocolia's office?

t_ch: 这你就不知道了，老弟——我俩当年的关系好到不分彼此，任何好东西都会拿来分享。
t_en: I don't think anyone realized, but back then we were so close we didn't even differentiate between each other's possessions. We shared pretty much everything.

t_ch: 我们住在同一间宿舍，每天一起上学，一起吃饭，一起打发无聊的时光…偶尔也会一起聊聊音乐——虽然她在那方面是个外行。
t_en: We lived in the same dormitory, went to the same school together every day, ate together, whiled away the time together... and occasionally talked about music together — though it wasn't something she was overly familiar with.

t_ch: 这种关系一直维持到了彻底决裂之前。直到闹翻的那天，她的办公室里还留着很多我们共用过的东西……
t_en: That's how things were until the day we fell out. A lot of our shared possessions never left her office again...

t_ch: 我一直在期待，或许有一天，她会把这把吉他还给我…等到那天，说不定我们就能冰释前嫌了。
t_en: I was always looking forward to the day she'd return this guitar... I thought that would be the day we could bury the hatchet.

t_ch: 现在想想…我还是太天真了。也许她早就忘了还有这么一样东西了。
t_en: Now that I think about it... perhaps I was too naive. She'd probably forgotten it even existed.

t_ch: …她可能比你想象得要更绝情。
t_en: ...She may have been colder than you thought to begin with.

t_ch: …你也这么觉得吗？
t_en: ...You think so too?

t_ch: 你没做错任何事，希露瓦。
t_en: You didn't do anything wrong, Serval.

t_ch: …我明白。但正是因为这点…我才更受打击。
t_en: *sigh* I know... but that's what makes it harder.

t_ch: 过去的就让它过去吧，姐姐。
t_en: Let bygones be bygones, sister.

t_ch: 嗐，话题怎么又沉重起来了，这可不行！还是说点有意思的吧。
t_en: How did the conversation get so heavy again!? No good — let's talk about something interesting.

t_ch: 知道吗老弟？你手上的「琴匣」，还有我的宝贝吉他…它们的原型其实都来自于这台「超电磁流射演奏器」。
t_en: You know something, Gepard? That guitar case in your hand, and my guitar... They're both from the same prototype — the "super electromagnetic flux player."

t_ch: 没想到这把吉他还能与我有所联系。真是奇妙的缘分……
t_en: I never knew your guitar had a connection to me too.... Quite the story.

t_ch: 这个小箱子里装的是什么？
t_en: What's in the case, Serval?

t_ch: 那个…我想想。啊，对了——是铁卫制式的随身暖炉来着，不过已经坏了。
t_en: Well... let's see. Ah, yes — a Silvermane Guard standard issue portable heater. I—It's broken, though.

t_ch: 那可是兰黛尔祖先留下来的传家宝…你多少也该爱护一下吧。
t_en: It's a family heirloom — it belonged to Rhonda once... You should take care of it.

t_ch: 嗐，我原本倒是想珍藏起来的…但你也知道，老古董都是这个毛病——越不用越容易坏。再说，修了它也不会有人给我报酬，对吧？
t_en: *sigh* I wanted to keep it with me... but that's the problem with old things — the less you use them, the easier they break. Besides, no one's paying me to fix it, right?

t_ch: 欸，对了！{NICKNAME}，你之前不是在我这学了一手维修机械的本事吗？要不要再拿这老古董练练手？
t_en: Hey, wait a minute! {NICKNAME}, didn't I teach you a few machine-overhaul skills? Wanna practice on this old antique?

t_ch: 你又来了……
t_en: Here we go again...

t_ch: 行啊，怎么修？
t_en: Sure. How do I fix it?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你不必对姐姐言听计从……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you don't have to do everything she tells you...

t_ch: 小杰杰…你平时也总被使唤吗？
t_en: Lil' Geppie... Did she order you around like this too?

t_ch: 罢了…我不想提及那段往事。
t_en: *sigh* ...Like I said, let bygones be bygones.

t_ch: …这东西的原理，和你第一次来机械屋时修的那玩意一样，看了就明白了！
t_en: ...The principle behind this machine and the one you fixed when you first came to the workshop is the same. You'll understand when you see it.

t_ch: 那就拜托你啦，{NICKNAME}。作为回报…我就给你讲讲这老古董的故事，如何？
t_en: So, I'll leave this one to you, {NICKNAME}. In return... how about I tell you the backstory behind it?

t_ch: 没问题，让我看看…
t_en: No problem, let me have a look...

t_ch: 我就知道你是最可靠的徒弟，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: I knew you were a reliable apprentice, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 你看我有得选吗？
t_en: Do I have a choice?

t_ch: 嘿，别这么委屈巴巴的嘛。
t_en: Hehe, come on, it isn't that difficult.

t_ch: 要不你还是别上车了…
t_en: Maybe we shouldn't let you join the Astral Express after all...

t_ch: 哎？！别别别——你看我这不是…腾不出手嘛。
t_en: What!? No! No, no, no — can't you see I'm just short of hands here?

t_ch: 你想啊，你第一次来这里的时候就帮我修了机器，这最后一次来也帮了忙…这就叫有始有终，对吧？
t_en: Think about it — you helped me fix my machine the first time you came here, and now you're helping me the last time too... That's called having a beginning and an end, right?

t_ch: …辛苦你陪姐姐再折腾一会儿了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Well done for putting up with her for this long, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哇哦——很利索嘛，{NICKNAME}！不愧是你。
t_en: Wow, great job, {NICKNAME}! Like I said — a reliable apprentice!

t_ch: 按刚才约定好的，我来给你讲讲这小暖炉背后的故事吧。
t_en: As promised, let me tell you the story behind this little heater.

t_ch: 小暖炉原本的主人，是我们朗道家的一位祖先，兰黛尔•朗道。她可是七百年前贝洛伯格保卫战里大放异彩的英雄之一。
t_en: Its original owner was an ancestor of the Landau family — Rhonda Landau. She was one of our great heroes who participated in the Defense of Belobog <unbreak>700</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 根据记载，主力决战的时候，她亲自率领两个铁卫旅，在城下与敌军大战了整整四个昼夜…当时的剪报我还留着呢，有机会给你看看。
t_en: The records tell that during the decisive battle, she led two Silvermane Guard brigades. She engaged the enemy for four full days and nights in the fight for the city... I still have the newspaper clippings from that time — I'll show you when I get the chance.

t_ch: 真的吗？好强…
t_en: She must have been pretty strong...

t_ch: 当然啦，毕竟是我们朗道家的祖先嘛！
t_en: Of course, after all, she was a Landau!

t_ch: 这剪报我好像捡到过…
t_en: I think I've seen the clippings before...

t_ch: 这…似乎不大可能，毕竟已经过去七百年了。
t_en: <unbreak>700</unbreak>-year-old newspaper clippings? That... seems a little unlikely.

t_ch: 不过姐姐的话你也别全信。就算真的有剪报，大概率也已经被她弄丢了。
t_en: I'd take that with a grain of salt. Even if they do exist, odds are she lost them.

t_ch: 剪报什么的才不是重点——我想告诉{NICKNAME}的是，我们朗道家人才辈出！
t_en: Ugh, the clippings aren't the point — what I'm trying to say to {NICKNAME} is that we have a lot of talent in the Landau family!

t_ch: 「为自己的选择负责，为贝洛伯格的人民负责」…这就是驱动朗道家每一个人不断向前的动力。
t_en: Mm — "take responsibility for your choices and the people of Belobog..." Every Landau is driven by that ethos.

t_ch: 这下应该都搞定了……
t_en: That should be everything...

t_ch: 呼…总算收拾完了。这行李恐怕不比从士官学校毕业时少……
t_en: Phew... finally. I think I must have left cadet school with about the same amount...

t_ch: 不容易。不过，姐姐，我想再问你一次……
t_en: No easy job. Serval, I want to ask you again...

t_ch: 你…真的要和「开拓者」一起离开贝洛伯格吗？
t_en: Do you... really want to leave Belobog and go with the Trailblazers?

t_ch: …当然啦！这可是一辈子只有一次的机会哪，我可不能错过了。
t_en: ...Of course! This is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity — I can't miss it.

t_ch: 怎么了，老弟？啊…我懂了——难道是舍不得姐姐我了？
t_en: What's wrong, Gepard? Ahh, I see... Can't let go of your dear ol' sister, eh?

t_ch: 我…我还是不发表看法了。我不想给你增加思想负担。
t_en: I... Forget it. I don't want to complicate things for you.

t_ch: 不过在你离开之前，我还有最后一事相求——戍卫队近期要去下层区一趟，勘察磐岩镇周边的情况。你也跟我一起去吧。
t_en: But before you leave, I have one last request to make. The Garrison is heading down to the Underworld soon to survey the situation around Boulder Town. Why don't you come with me?

t_ch: …欸？你上半句才说不想给我增加负担，怎么这就给我派上任务了……
t_en: ...Huh? You just said you didn't wanna complicate things for me, and now you're sending me on a mission...

t_ch: 啊～我明白了——你是想再好好珍惜一下有姐姐陪在身边的时光，对吧？
t_en: Ahaa~ You just wanna spend as much time as possible with me before I go, right?

t_ch: 不，不是…我听说下层区有很多老旧的能源中枢，整个贝洛伯格恐怕只有你能给那些装置做彻底的检查了。
t_en: N—No, it's not that... I've heard there are a lot of old energy hubs in the Underworld, and you're probably the only person in Belobog who knows how to inspect them.

t_ch: 既然你横竖都要走，不如在那之前再帮城里的人民一个忙。
t_en: Since you're leaving one way or the other, why don't you do the people of the city one last favor before you go?

t_ch: 都到这个时候了，你脑袋里装的还都是「人民」啊…真不知道该说什么好。
t_en: Even at a time like this, you're still thinking about the people... *sigh* I don't know what to say.

t_ch: 行吧，那我就帮你这个忙——朗道姐弟第一次、也是最后一次通力合作！
t_en: Alright, I'll help you out — the first and last time the Landau siblings will work together!

t_ch: 谢谢。对了，还有{NICKNAME}——如果时间允许的话，能否也请你随铁卫同去？
t_en: Thanks, sis. {NICKNAME}, if you have the time, could you accompany the Garrison?

t_ch: 你毕竟是拯救了贝洛伯格的英雄，在下层区颇有人望。如果有你陪同开路，多少能减轻铁卫重新入驻下层的阻力。
t_en: You're Belobog's hero — I'm sure you must be popular in the Underworld. The Silvermane Guards will face less resistance if you come with us.

t_ch: 当然，记得提前短信联系我。
t_en: Of course, remember to send me a message in advance.

t_ch: 谢谢。等铁卫这边的准备工作做好，我就联系你。
t_en: Thanks. I'll contact you when the Guards have finished their preparations.

t_ch: 啊…收拾累了，今天就到这里吧。辛苦你们俩陪我忙前忙后啦。
t_en: *sigh* ...I'm tired of packing. Let's leave it here for today. Thanks for your help.

t_ch: 呵…明明喝了那么多茶，怎么还是这么快就困了……
t_en: *yawn* I drank all that tea. Why am I getting sleepy?

t_ch: 我等下就要回军区了。{NICKNAME}，你要一起吗？
t_en: I'm heading back to the military zone later. {NICKNAME}, do you want to go with me?

t_ch: 你怎么看希露瓦这件事？
t_en: What do you think about this whole matter with Serval?

t_ch: 嗯？你要是问我怎么看……
t_en: Huh? Hmm. If you want my opinion...

t_ch: 我只能说，说走就走确实是她的作风。她一直这么任性，每次都心安理得地给人添麻烦……
t_en: All I can say is that it's in character for her to say something and then do it without thinking. She's always been impulsive, and she doesn't mind creating a headache or two along the way...

t_ch: …如果她能在和你们的旅行中改掉这个毛病的话，那我想也算是值了吧。
t_en: ...If she can remedy that habit during her trip with you, then I guess it will have been worth it.

t_ch: 你其实很舍不得她吧。
t_en: You're sad to see her go, aren't you?

t_ch: 看来还是藏不住啊……
t_en: Turns out I'm no good at hiding it...

t_ch: 哎，那毕竟是和我一起从小到大的亲姐姐。如果说舍得…那肯定是假的。
t_en: *sigh* She's my sister — we grew up together. I'd be lying if I said I'm not going to miss her.

t_ch: 我倒也不是怕会寂寞——毕竟有你们送的那个…呃，通讯装置。如果真想她的话，我随时发短信就好了。
t_en: I'm not afraid of being alone. After all, there's that, uh... communication device you gave me. If I really miss her, I can send her a message whenever I want.

t_ch: 我只是担心她独自一人踏上旅程，能不能好好照顾自己。不过，有你这样优秀的人作旅伴，我的担心大概都是多余的吧。
t_en: It's more that I'm worried she won't look after herself properly... Then again, with capable people like you as travel companions, I'm probably overthinking things.

t_ch: 不过，有你这样优秀的人作她的旅伴，我的担心大概都是多余的吧。
t_en: However, with someone as capable as you as her traveling companion, all my worries are probably superfluous.

t_ch: 总之，姐姐她就拜托你了。
t_en: Basically, I'm trusting you to take good care of my sister.

t_ch: 能和她一起旅行，我也很开心。
t_en: I'm happy to be able to travel with her. It won't be a chore.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你…确实是个很可靠的人。她能受你这样优秀的旅伴照顾，就连我也感到很幸运。
t_en: You're... a very reliable person, {NICKNAME}. Even I feel lucky to have you as her travel companion.

t_ch: 不过，你可别小看了姐姐她折腾人的能力…如果你们什么时候实在受不了了，就把她送回来吧。
t_en: Still, don't underestimate her ability to drive people crazy... If at some point your crew can't take it anymore, just send her back.

t_ch: 这世界上可能也就我和佩拉受得了她了…哦，还有她的那位助手——茉莉小姐。
t_en: Pela and I are probably the only ones in the world who know how to handle her... oh, and that assistant of hers too — Molly.

t_ch: 时候不早了，我们准备动身吧。或者，你还想聊些什么吗？
t_en: It's getting late, let's get ready to move. Is there something else you wanted to talk about?

t_ch: 那我们等下一起走吧。
t_en: We should get ready to head out.

t_ch: 没问题。等你收拾好，我们就一起出发。
t_en: Agreed — as soon as you're packed.

t_ch: 哦？这不是{NICKNAME}嘛。怎么，你不去忙吗？
t_en: Oh? Well look, it's {NICKNAME}. You don't have any business you need to attend to?

t_ch: 要是不忙就在我这机械屋多坐会儿吧，别客气。
t_en: If you're not busy, relax at my workshop for a while longer. Make yourself at home.

t_ch: 你真的下定决心要离开了吗？
t_en: Have you really made up your mind to leave?

t_ch: 是啊，我做了好久思想准备呢。要是这会儿再反悔，那我不是亏大了嘛～
t_en: Yes, I've been preparing myself mentally for a long time. If I back out now, it'll be a huge loss.

t_ch: 你舍得让杰帕德一个人留着吗？
t_en: Can you bear to leave Gepard here by himself?

t_ch: 嗯…如果我说舍得，你会信吗？
t_en: Well... would you believe me if I said yes?

t_ch: 哈哈，那可是我亲生弟弟啊，怎么会舍得扔下他一个人走呢？不过…我也别无选择就是了。
t_en: Haha, he's my little brother, of course I don't want to leave him alone. But... I don't have any other choice.

t_ch: 话说回来，我又不是永远不回来了。再者，实在想他的话…我发短信骚扰他不就好了？
t_en: Also, i—it's not like I'm never coming back. And if I really miss him... I can just pester the heck outta him over the phone, right?

t_ch: 想联系的话总有办法啦，不用担心！
t_en: There's always a way for me to contact him if I need to, so don't worry!

t_ch: 你想走的原因没那么简单吧？
t_en: The reason you want to leave isn't so simple, is it?

t_ch: 嗯…还是被你看出来了。
t_en: Hm... you can see right through me.

t_ch: 不过，这其中的原因我并不想和人说…还希望你能多理解一下啦。
t_en: I'm sorry, I'm not ready to talk to anyone about the reasons behind my decision... I hope you can understand.

t_ch: 好吧，我尊重你的选择。
t_en: Okay, I respect your choice.

t_ch: 哈哈，谢谢你。今后也请你多关照啦。
t_en: Haha, thank you. Make sure you look after me now~

t_ch: 我还有事，先告辞了。
t_en: I have things to do, so I'll leave you to it.

t_ch: 好嘞。要是有什么其他状况，你随时短信联系我就行。
t_en: Okay. If anything comes up, you can always message me.

t_ch: 希露瓦的吉他
t_en: Serval's guitar

t_ch: 抽屉
t_en: Drawer

t_ch: 神秘的植物标本
t_en: Mysterious plant specimens

t_ch: 未完成的信
t_en: Unfinished letter

t_ch: 这就是你们现在认识的那位可可利亚——永远走在所有人的前面，永远不回头看哪怕一眼……
t_en: And that's the only Cocolia you guys ever got to know, huh... Always ahead of everyone else, never looking back, even for a second...

t_ch: …把我、还有与有关我的所有记忆，全都抛在脑后。
t_en: ...She put it all out of her mind. Me, and every memory to do with me...

t_ch: 姐姐的东西…比我的还乱啊。
t_en: ...And I thought I was messy.

t_ch: （杰帕德似乎在专心收拾行李……）
t_en: (Looks like Gepard's got his work cut out for him...)

t_ch: 你来了，{NICKNAME}。辛苦你跑这一趟。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you're here. Thanks again for agreeing to this.

t_ch: 不好意思，让你们久等了。
t_en: Sorry to keep you waiting.

t_ch: 没什么，其实我们也才到不久。
t_en: Don't be, I only just arrived myself.

t_ch: 我猜你们也刚到不久…
t_en: You look like you only just got here...

t_ch: 你猜得对，我们确实刚到。
t_en: You're not wrong, it's only been a few minutes.

t_ch: 我还以为你会带大部队过来呢。
t_en: What about the rest of the Garrison?

t_ch: 大部队在后面。如果交涉顺利的话，邓恩会负责带队开进下层区的。
t_en: They're behind us. If negotiations go well, then Dunn will lead them down into the Underworld.

t_ch: 邓恩是个能力很强的士官。他为人刚正不阿，是铁卫的模范。
t_en: He's a very capable sergeant — upright, plainspoken — a model Silvermane Guard.

t_ch: ——你应该没忘记他吧？他之前在铁卫禁区被你和希露瓦围…制服，昏迷了许久才醒。
t_en: ...I assume you remember him? You and Serval ambushed — uh... subdued him in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone. I think he was out cold for most of the action.

t_ch: 我不是故意的…
t_en: It was an accident...

t_ch: 那时你们同他爆发冲突是形势所迫，我能理解。
t_en: The conflict was a product of the situation, I understand.

t_ch: 况且…他没有落下大碍，能看出你们有刻意留手。
t_en: Not to mention... he wasn't in bad shape when we found him. Thank you for holding back.

t_ch: 不打不相识嘛！
t_en: Sometimes fights lead to friendship!

t_ch: 那时你们同他爆发冲突是形势所迫，我能理解。
t_en: The conflict was a product of the situation, I understand.

t_ch: 况且…他没有落下大碍，能看出你们有刻意留手。
t_en: Not to mention... he wasn't in bad shape when we found him. Thank you for holding back.

t_ch: 往事不必再提…
t_en: What was that "bygones" thing you said again...?

t_ch: 只能说，多亏你们刻意留手，没有让他落下大碍。
t_en: He wasn't in bad shape when we found him. Thank you for holding back.

t_ch: 这已经是最好的结果了，你们不必挂心。
t_en: Don't worry, the hard work is already behind us.

t_ch: 我稍后要和「地火」的首领进行交涉，这是今天行程的重中之重。虽然希露瓦那边稍微耽误了一阵，但不影响后续安排。我们出发吧。
t_en: The highest priority for today is establishing good negotiations with the chief of Wildfire — I'm about to get that process underway. Serval was a little delayed on the way over, but that doesn't affect our schedule. Let's set off.

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Alrighty.

t_ch: 慢着慢着，我可不想被你当成不靠谱的人啊……
t_en: Hold up, I wasn't that late...

t_ch: 希露瓦？我就知道…
t_en: Huh, classic Serval.

t_ch: 哎？慢着，我可不想被你当成不靠谱的人啊……
t_en: Huh? Hold up, I wasn't that late...

t_ch: 虽然姐姐平时就没什么时间观念…但今天的情况确实特殊，就不怪她了。那么久没回家看爸妈…能多聊聊也好。
t_en: You weren't that early, either... Still, I don't blame you — you hadn't seen Mom and Dad in a long time... I'm glad you managed to speak to them.

t_ch: 其实也没聊那么多…你懂的，跟老爸聊天总会让我一肚子火。
t_en: We didn't speak all that much... You know how it is, I can only talk to Dad for so long before I start losing it.

t_ch: 我大概明白。暂时放下你心里的不愉快吧…我们该出发了。
t_en: I know how it is alright. Put that to one side for now... We need to get going.

t_ch: 会谈的地点是磐岩镇的「诊所」。我和希露瓦都是初来乍到，得麻烦{NICKNAME}你带路了。
t_en: Negotiations will be held in the Boulder Town clinic. Serval and I are newcomers, so you'll have to lead the way, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 希露瓦，我问过了…
t_en: Serval, I already asked...

t_ch: 我们接下来做什么？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 接下来该做什么？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 这只是个装饰，它的转动不产生任何效果。
t_en: This is just a decoration, its rotation does not produce any effect.

t_ch: 这些转动的齿轮会不会在给什么供能？比如说，也许机械屋会站起来，变成巨大机器人？
t_en: Could these spinning gears be powering something? For example, maybe the workshop will stand up and turn into a giant robot?

t_ch: 有幸待在室内的行李架。
t_en: The luggage rack that has the privilege of staying indoors.

t_ch: 这座走廊灯底部装有加热系统，设计非常聪明。
t_en: This hallway light has a very clever design and is equipped with a heating system at the bottom.

t_ch: 即使在白天，这条走廊依然灯火通明。
t_en: Even during the daytime, this corridor is still brightly lit.

t_ch: 旅店内部非常暖和，有家的温馨。
t_en: The interior of the hostel is nice and cozy.
